stem: When a user is on a network, is this a situation that would prevent Desktop Single Sign-On (SSO) from signing the user in automatically?

options.text: The IIS Server is not available
The user does not have a valid Kerberos ticket

stem: An Okta Administrator needs to enable Active Directory mastered users to change passwords in Okta. Is this a step that the administrator needs to take?

options.text: Set the Active Directory recovery options to true
Install the Okta Password Sync Agent
Ensure the Okta service account has elevated permissions in Active Directory

stem: Is this the default date range for which events are displayed in the Okta system log?
Users are unable to authenticate to Microsoft OneNote when using Desktop SSO/IWA. Is this a likely cause of the issue the users are experiencing?

options.text: Desktop SSO/IWA does NOT work with OneNote
Desktop SSO/IWA was NOT deployed in HTTPS mode

stem: Is this mapping type available when an administrator maps Okta attributes to Active Directory attributes?

options.text: Apply mapping on user create only

stem: Is this information contained in a SAML assertion?

Last week, an Okta administrator configured Okta to use Active Directory (AD) for Delegated Authentication. This week, a new Active Directory user is unable to log on to Okta.
Is this a reason why the new Active Directory user might be unable to authenticate?

options.text: The user is outside of an Active Directory Organizational Unit (OU) that has been imported into Okta.
The user was not successfully imported into Okta from Active Directory.
The user account is disabled, or the user is locked out.

stem:
An Okta Administrator is creating a custom attribute in Okta named "Initials" to streamline employees' experience with DocuSign. The Initials field must be populated when data is mapped from Active Directory to Okta.
Is this the Okta Expression Language function the administrator should use to complete the task?
Is this an example of a provisioning operation that Okta can perform in an application?

options.text: Delete all user passwords in an applciation
Import a password from an application

stem:
A company has the following environment:
  • All employees and their group memberships are in an Active Directory domain.
  • A Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP) server contains all of the company's external users (customers and partners).
  • All of the employee user accounts are replicated in the LDAP server from Active Directory.
  • All company employees, customers, and partners need access to certain applications through Okta.
< r>The company has integrated both Active Directory and LDAP with Okta.
Is this a valid method to resolve the user duplication arising from the employee accounts existing in both Active Directory and LDAP?

options.text: If the employee, customer, and partner accounts are stored in different Organizational Units (OUs) in the LDAP server, then Okta can be configured to exclude the employee OU. This action will prevent the collision with the same users also being imported from Active Directory.
